ocr_text:
郭 Shell(guo)
郭使弩機各零件更緊密連結，並避免直接接觸到弩臂，加固弩臂的機槽。最初的弩機並無郭，秦代開始出現帶郭的弩機，漢代起普遍使用有郭的弩機。
The guo prevents direct contact between the crossbow mechanism parts and the crossbow stock, reinforcing the groove of the crossbow stock. The early crossbow mechanisms did not have a guo. Crossbows with '郭' began to appear in the Qin Dynasty, and widely used from the Han Dynasty onwards.
caption: 弩機部件「郭」的圖解說明，搭配青銅弩機實物局部。
notes: 弩機構造解說系列之一

ocr_text:
樞 Bolts (shu)
樞有兩件，用於固定。一是固定弩機各部件的位置，二是將整個弩機固定於弩臂凹槽中。
The shu, two in total, are used for fixation, serving two functions: first, securing the positions of crossbow mechanism parts, and second, anchoring the entire crossbow mechanism in the groove of the crossbow stock.
caption: 弩機部件「樞」的圖解說明，搭配青銅弩機實物局部。
notes: 弩機構造解說系列之一

gallery_guess: 玉劍飾（君子服劍主題牆）
ocr_text:
君子服劍 A Nobleman Wears a Sword
「帶長鋏之陸離兮，冠切雲之崔嵬，被明月兮珮寶璐。」 —屈原《九章·涉江》
腰佩光彩的長劍，頭戴高聳的帽子，背披夜明珠，腰佩美玉
At my belt a long sword swinging, on my head a cleave-cloud hat up-towering, round my neck moon-bright jewels, and a precious jade at my girdle. -Qu Yuan, Crossing the River, from Nine Pieces
屈原透過這段外在形象的自我描述，表達出其志高潔、不願同流合汙之內在情感。讀者恰可從中了解一位儀表堂堂的貴族該有的行頭。其中，服劍是不可或缺。
Through this self-description of his external image, Qu Yuan expresses his noble and unsullied aspirations and an inner sentiment of not wanting to conform or be tainted by the mainstream. Readers can discern from this statement the dignified appearance expected of a nobleman. Among these, wearing a sword is indispensable.
改繪自戰國中晚期「人物御龍帛畫圖」（湖南省博物館藏）
根據三國時代的魏國孟康，所謂的玉具劍是指「摽、首、鐔、衛，盡以玉為之。」玉具劍是漢代最高規格的劍，不僅是王公貴族的配飾，也曾作為外交禮物由漢代皇帝餽贈給匈奴首領。
A sword with jade fittings has its scabbard chape, pommel, cross-guard, and scabbard slide made of jade. These jade-adorned swords are ranked the highest among all kinds of swords in the Han Dynasty. They served not only as accessories for royalty and nobility but also as diplomatic gifts presented by the Han emperors to the Xiongnu leaders.
pommel 劍首 / cross-guard 劍鐔(格) / scabbard slide 劍鼻(璏) / scabbard chape 劍摽(珌)
caption: 「君子服劍」展區主題牆，引用屈原《涉江》詩句並附玉具劍構造圖。
notes: 展區大段介紹牆

ocr_text:
5 引 清 緬甸密支那 山產輝玉料 Myitkyina area, Myanmar. Qing dynasty Jadeite 玉9578
在大自然中，輝玉比閃玉來得稀少。緬甸北部密支那地區所產的輝玉，常因含有鉻、鐵等元素的離子而呈現翠綠與褐紅，俗稱這樣鮮豔的輝玉為「翡翠」。這塊翠玉瓊已被切開一塊，可以看見在粗糙的圍岩裡面包裹著鮮豔的翠玉。
Jadeite is much more rarely available in nature than nephrite. The output from Myitkyina in the northern Myanmar often acquires a dazzling shade of emerald green or brownish red from chromium and iron ions, respectively. Jadeite of such bright colors is commonly known as feicui (kingfisher feathers). The specimen displayed is partially cut, revealing beautiful jadeite hidden beneath the rugged wall rock.
caption: 展櫃陳列緬甸密支那山產輝玉（翡翠）原石一塊，部分切開露出翠綠玉肉，標號5。
notes: 玉料原石展示，輝玉/翡翠

ocr_text:
1 現代 台灣花蓮豐田 山產閃玉料 Fongtian, Hualien County, Taiwan. Modern-era. Nephrite 普贈205
從超基性火成岩變質而成的閃玉，常呈不太均勻的綠色，在這樣的閃玉內常含有小黑點狀的鉻鐵礦或石墨等雜質。這塊玉料採自台灣花蓮豐田，類似的閃玉也產於新疆天山北麓瑪納斯，以及加拿大英屬哥倫比亞等地。
Nephrite formed metasomatically from ultramafic igneous rocks often comes in uneven shades of green and with impurities in small black dots of chromite or graphite. The unworked nephrite displayed here is mined from Hualien (Taiwan). Deposits of similar nephrite are also found in Manas at the northern foothill of Tian Mountains (Xinjiang, China), and British Columbia (Canada).
2 現代 新疆且末 山產閃玉料 ...3、4 ...
（右側部分）2 現代 新疆且末 山產閃玉料 ...從含鎂...新疆南部的崑崙山藏...3、4 ...局部裂隙...又受氧化鐵...
caption: 展櫃陳列閃玉（軟玉）原料：兩件鵝卵石狀玉籽與綠色片狀山料，標號1至4。
notes: 閃玉原料展示，編號1-4，含台灣花蓮與新疆料

artifact_name_zh: 山產閃玉料 / 閃玉籽料
ocr_text:
2 現代 新疆且末 山產閃玉料 ...from Hualien ...British Columbia. Modern-era. ...green and with ...graphite. The ...also found in ...Mountains (Xinjiang,
從含鎂的鎂質碳酸鹽岩接觸變質而成的閃玉，常呈比較均勻的白或青白色，若有石墨侵入，則會變成灰色或墨黑色。如果有氧化鐵滲入，就會形成深淺不同的褐紅色皮。新疆南部的崑崙山藏豐富的這類閃玉。
3、4 清 新疆和闐 二件 閃玉籽料 故玉8618、故玉2930
2號玉料是且末地區的山產玉料，用鎚擊方式打成長塊形。3、4是和闐地區河床中撈拾的籽料，經水濶沙磨，表面已光滑溫潤。又受氧化鐵滲入而呈淺黃至深褐色。
caption: 展櫃陳列新疆且末山產閃玉與和闐閃玉籽料，標號1、2白色山料立置。
notes: IMG_4551同組閃玉展櫃，聚焦編號2山料與和闐籽料

artifact_name_zh: 山產閃玉料 / 閃玉籽料
ocr_text:
Qiemo, Xinjiang, China. Modern-era. Nephrite On Loan from private collection
Nephrite resulted from contact metamorphism of Mg-calcium carbonate rocks is generally of even whiteness or with a bluish tint. Permeation by graphite would turn gray or dark black, while ferric oxide gives it a brownish red skin in various shades. Struck by human hands into its current rectangular shape, the No.2 piece exhibited here comes from the Kunlun Mountains, south of Xinjiang, where rich deposits exist. The other two pebbles No.3&4 are gathered from the riverbeds in Khotan; the soft natural polish is the result of the abrasion force of water. There are also some cracks through which iron oxide has permeated, showing tinctures of light yellow to dark brown.
Khotan, Xinjiang, China. Qing dynasty Nephrite Pebbles
